summary: Plan-only PR inventory classification for 16 open PRs. The job is not a dedupe cluster, has no shared canonical, and merge/fix/raise_pr/low-signal closeout are disabled, so recommendations are conservative and non-mutating.

actions.reason: Independent PR inventory item; no duplicate/superseded evidence and merge is blocked by job frontmatter.
Independent feature/fix PR; bot review presence blocks merge preflight but not non-mutating classification.
Independent channel bugfix; no duplicate/superseded evidence and proof status still calls for normal maintainer review.
Independent UI bugfix; no close or merge action is allowed by this plan job.
Independent draft PR with failed proof gates; keep open for author/proof path rather than closing in an inventory shard.
Independent maintainer-owned replacement PR; not a dedupe closeout target and security routing is not indicated by the artifact.
Independent Slack behavior/config PR; no duplicate/superseded evidence and merge is disabled.
Independent bugfix PR with conflicts; keep open rather than close in this inventory shard.
Maintainer/product judgment is required because the hydrated author review raises compatibility/product-intent tradeoffs for changing insecure Control UI auth defaults.
Independent larger ACP PR; no closure basis and merge/fix actions are disabled.
Independent dependency update; no duplicate/superseded evidence and merge is disabled.
Independent small bugfix PR; partial checks enrichment gap does not require maintainer escalation for non-mutating classification.
Independent dependency update; no route_security signal in artifact and no merge action allowed.
Independent read-tool feature/fix PR; checks enrichment gap blocks merge-style decisions only, not conservative classification.
Independent broad gateway feature PR; no closure basis, and checks hydration gap plus job frontmatter prevent merge/fix planning.
Independent session-state bugfix PR; no duplicate/superseded evidence and merge is disabled.

needs_human: #88798 requires maintainer/product judgment on whether changing generated insecure Control UI auth defaults is acceptable given the hydrated author review's compatibility concern.
